text.title: Web Scraping Pagination Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure from "we need to walk a paginated source" to "walk-report emitted with high-water mark". Decision-gates block on common failure modes.

step.name: detect-pattern
step.action: Inspect the listing page. If a `[rel=next]` or pagination-button is present → next-button. If `IntersectionObserver` or `scrollHeight` polling drives loading → infinite-scroll. If a visible "Load more" button toggles items → load-more.
step.decision-gate: If pattern is ambiguous — STOP, fetch two sample pages and re-classify.

step.name: cap-pool
step.action: Initialise `p-limit(cap)` (TS) or `asyncio.Semaphore(cap)` (Py). Wrap every page open in try/finally with close in finally.
step.decision-gate: If cap > 10 without infra justification — STOP, the rate-limit risk exceeds the speed-up.

step.name: walk
step.action: Run the walker. Per-pattern stop condition: next-button → !exists OR disabled; infinite-scroll → scrollHeight stable for 4 s; load-more → button absent OR out-of-viewport. Hard ceiling max_pages=500.
step.decision-gate: If pages_walked reaches max_pages — STOP, walker likely buggy.

step.name: dedupe
step.input: raw item stream, dedupe key (id / URL / sha1 of normalized fields).
step.action: Pass each item through a `seen: Set<key>`. If seen, increment duplicate_count and drop. Else append to results.
step.decision-gate: If duplicate_count=0 on infinite-scroll with >50 items — STOP, dedupe is mis-keyed.

step.name: emit-report
step.action: Compose walk-report: pattern, pages_walked, items_collected, duplicate_count, concurrency_cap, pool_high_water_mark, stop_condition_observed.
